currentTask.context: Designing Game 39: Last Rope Standing - pendulum grapple survival, rising floodwaters

gameQueue.title: Last Rope Standing
gameQueue.concept: Grappling hook survival: the last outlaw standing swings between fence posts on a rope lasso as floodwaters rise. Tap to latch onto the nearest post, pendulum-swing to gain height, release to fly to the next anchor point. Miss an anchor and you drop. First grapple-physics mechanic in the series.
gameQueue.raisesBarOn: First pendulum-swing grapple physics in series, first vertical survival structure with rising threat, first game where spatial awareness and swing momentum are the primary skills

gameQueue.title: Wire Tap
gameQueue.concept: Telegraph Morse code decoder: intercept incoming messages from the outlaw gang. Each message is a sequence of short/long audio pulses (dots and dashes). Tap to mark dots vs dashes in real time, decode the letter, then identify the outlaw's destination on a frontier map. Race against the clock before the signal cuts out.
gameQueue.raisesBarOn: First temporal pattern-decoding mechanic in series, first Morse code / telegraph communication game, first game where listening and timing decode information rather than react to threats

gameQueue.title: Gold Panner
gameQueue.concept: Skill-based prospecting: tilt a gold pan (left/right drag) to slosh water and separate gold flakes from gravel. Each round introduces more mud and lighter fools-gold that moves like real gold. Physics-based fluid slosh model with particle stratification by density. Race against drying pan and claim jumpers.
gameQueue.raisesBarOn: First fluid simulation mechanic in series, first density/stratification physics, first game based on manual dexterity over a sustained object rather than reaction timing
